notes: Wide corpus. Register field carries methodological weight. Run analyses on full corpus AND authorship_confidence >= 0.95 subset. Compare results.

documents.title: Address Before the Young Men's Lyceum of Springfield, Illinois
documents.notes: Earliest major speech. Proto-covenant and proto-organism metaphors before Lincoln has political stakes — valuable baseline.
documents.source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.1 pp.108-115
documents.inclusion_rationale: Earliest major speech. Proto-covenant and proto-organism metaphors before Lincoln has political stakes — valuable baseline.
known_limitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

documents.title: Temperance Address

documents.inclusion_rationale: Body/disease cluster in alcohol reform context — establishes pre-political use of organic metaphors.

documents.title: Farewell Address at Springfield, Illinois
documents.authorship_notes: Two versions exist: extemporaneous transcription and Lincoln's written reconstruction. Analyze both.
documents.notes: Providence cluster in unguarded form. Lincoln speaking extemporaneously about his own death.
documents.source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.4 pp.190-191
documents.inclusion_rationale: Providence cluster in unguarded form. Lincoln speaking extemporaneously about his own death.

documents.title: First Inaugural Address
documents.authorship_notes: Seward drafted closing paragraphs ('mystic chords'). Tag seward_revised sentences. Run analysis twice: full and lincoln-sole-only.
documents.notes: Seward drafted 'mystic chords' closing. Tag seward_revised sentences. Run analysis twice: full and lincoln-sole-only.
documents.source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.4 pp.262-271

documents.title: Message to Congress in Special Session, July 4, 1861
documents.notes: Experiment/proposition most explicit here. Note how metaphors operate in administrative register.
documents.source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.4 pp.421-441
documents.inclusion_rationale: Experiment/proposition most explicit here. Note how metaphors operate in administrative register.
known_limitations: Administrative register constrains metaphor density and should be controlled before comparing to formal addresses.

documents.title: Fragment on the Constitution and the Union
documents.authorship_notes: Date uncertain; probably January 1861. Private note with no intended audience.
documents.notes: Lincoln privately reasons Union is 'picture of silver', liberty the 'apple of gold'. Rare explicit metaphor theorizing. High epistemic value.
documents.source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.4 pp.168-169
documents.inclusion_rationale: Lincoln privately reasons Union is 'picture of silver', liberty the 'apple of gold'. Rare explicit metaphor theorizing. High epistemic value.
known_limitations: Private fragment with uncertain date; diachronic placement should be treated cautiously.
Private fragment has limited audience context and short length; frequency claims require caution.

documents.title: Letter to Horace Greeley
documents.notes: CRITICAL. 'If I could save the Union without freeing any slave I would do it.' Metaphor suppression event. Written 2 months before Preliminary Emancipation. Track covenant-over-freedom suppression.
documents.source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.5 pp.388-389
documents.inclusion_rationale: CRITICAL. 'If I could save the Union without freeing any slave I would do it.' Metaphor suppression event. Written 2 months before Preliminary Emancipation. Track covenant-over-freedom suppression.
known_limitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

documents.title: Preliminary Emancipation Proclamation
documents.notes: Deliberately flat. Absence of birth/body clusters is the finding. Use as control document.
documents.source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.5 pp.433-436
documents.inclusion_rationale: Deliberately flat. Absence of birth/body clusters is the finding. Use as control document.
known_limitations: Legal register intentionally suppresses metaphor; absence findings should be interpreted as register-controlled evidence, not as source failure.

documents.title: Annual Message to Congress, December 1, 1862
documents.notes: 'Last best hope of earth' — experiment frame at high intensity in administrative register.
documents.source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.5 pp.518-537
documents.inclusion_rationale: 'Last best hope of earth' — experiment frame at high intensity in administrative register.
known_limitations: Administrative register constrains metaphor density and should be controlled before comparing to formal addresses.

documents.title: Emancipation Proclamation
documents.notes: Compare to preliminary — same flatness. The document actually freeing enslaved people contains almost no metaphor. Flatness is data.
documents.source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.6 pp.28-30
documents.inclusion_rationale: Compare to preliminary — same flatness. The document actually freeing enslaved people contains almost no metaphor. Flatness is data.
known_limitations: Legal register intentionally suppresses metaphor; absence findings should be interpreted as register-controlled evidence, not as source failure.

documents.title: Letter to James C. Conkling
documents.notes: Written to be read aloud at rally. Defends emancipation to war Democrats. Track obligatory frame distribution across racial lines.
documents.source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.6 pp.406-410
documents.inclusion_rationale: Written to be read aloud at rally. Defends emancipation to war Democrats. Track obligatory frame distribution across racial lines.

documents.title: Address Delivered at the Dedication of the Cemetery at Gettysburg
documents.authorship_notes: Use Bliss copy as authoritative text.
documents.notes: 272 words. Birth, proposition, sacrifice, ancestral-debt all present. Highest metaphor density. Use Bliss copy. Structurally a birth narrative — 'brought forth' in line 1 reprised in 'new birth of freedom'. Sacrificial economy fully operational.
documents.source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.7 pp.22-23
documents.inclusion_rationale: 272 words. Birth, proposition, sacrifice, ancestral-debt all present. Highest metaphor density. Use Bliss copy. Structurally a birth narrative — 'brought forth' in line 1 reprised in 'new birth of freedom'. Sacrificial economy fully operational.
known_limitations: Multiple manuscript witnesses exist; variant-sensitive claims should identify the copy used.

documents.title: Memorandum Concerning His Probable Failure of Re-election
documents.notes: Private note sealed from cabinet. Providence cluster in purely private form. Key test: does public providence rhetoric reflect genuine private cognition?
documents.source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.7 pp.514-515
documents.inclusion_rationale: Private note sealed from cabinet. Providence cluster in purely private form. Key test: does public providence rhetoric reflect genuine private cognition?
known_limitations: Private fragment has limited audience context and short length; frequency claims require caution.

documents.title: Letter to Albert G. Hodges
documents.notes: 'I claim not to have controlled events but confess plainly that events have controlled me.' Obligatory frame and lincoln_non_agent as Lincoln's explicit self-description. Canonical. Also proto-Second-Inaugural theodicy.
documents.source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.7 pp.281-283
documents.inclusion_rationale: 'I claim not to have controlled events but confess plainly that events have controlled me.' Obligatory frame and lincoln_non_agent as Lincoln's explicit self-description. Canonical. Also proto-Second-Inaugural theodicy.
known_limitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

documents.notes: Extemporaneous. Experiment/proof frame unguarded — election result as empirical evidence.

documents.title: Second Inaugural Address
documents.notes: All clusters present. Providence/theodicy at full development. Distributed guilt. 'The Almighty has His own purposes.' Obligatory frame at maximum. Wound-and-healing closes. Diachronic endpoint.
documents.source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.8 pp.332-333
documents.inclusion_rationale: All clusters present. Providence/theodicy at full development. Distributed guilt. 'The Almighty has His own purposes.' Obligatory frame at maximum. Wound-and-healing closes. Diachronic endpoint.
known_limitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

documents.title: Last Public Address
documents.notes: Reconstruction policy. Wound-and-healing now programmatic. Diachronic endpoint for organism cluster. Delivered 4 days before assassination.
documents.source_edition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition; Collected Works vol.8 pp.399-405
documents.inclusion_rationale: Reconstruction policy. Wound-and-healing now programmatic. Diachronic endpoint for organism cluster. Delivered 4 days before assassination.
